skill.name: image-to-video
skill.description: Animate any still image on RunComfy — this skill is a smart router that matches the user's intent to the right i2v model in the RunComfy catalog. Picks HappyHorse 1.0 I2V (Arena #1, native audio, identity preservation) for general animations, Wan 2.7 with `audio_url` for custom-voiceover lip-sync, or Seedance 2.0 Pro for multi-modal animation from image + reference video + reference audio.
skill.summary: Intent-based router for image-to-video animation across RunComfy i2v models
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The 91 backtick detections are Markdown inline code formatting in documentation, not shell execution. Network URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints. Path traversal warnings are documentation placeholders. Credential storage uses proper file permissions (0600). CLI does not shell-expand prompts, preventing injection. No malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: External Command References (False Positive)
low_findings.description: 91 backtick detections are Markdown code formatting (e.g., `runcomfy run`), not shell execution. This is a documentation file with embedded CLI examples in code blocks. No actual command injection surface exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in Markdown files are standard inline code syntax. The skill document uses `runcomfy run` as a reference to CLI command names, not as executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs (Expected Behavior)
low_findings.description: 12 URL detections point to legitimate RunComfy service endpoints: model-api.runcomfy.net, happyhorse models, wan-ai, bytedance/seedance. These are documented external services that the skill routes to.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are RunComfy-owned domains documented in the skill. The skill explicitly whitelists these endpoints in the Security section.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Placeholders (Not Exploitable)
low_findings.description: The <absolute/path> placeholder in --output-dir examples is a documentation template. Users substitute their own paths. No directory traversal vulnerability exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Placeholder text <absolute/path> is standard documentation convention. The CLI accepts user-provided paths, which is expected behavior.

content.user_title: Animate Images into Videos with AI Models
content.value_statement: Converting static images to animated videos requires selecting the right AI model for each use case. This skill routes your request to HappyHorse, Wan 2.7, or Seedance based on whether you need identity preservation, lip-sync to voiceover, or multi-modal animation.
actual_capabilities: Routes image-to-video requests to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V (identity-stable portrait and product animation)
Supports Wan 2.7 with audio_url for lip-syncing to custom voiceover tracks
Enables Seedance 2.0 Pro for multi-modal animation combining image + reference video + reference audio
Handles aspect ratio, resolution, duration, and seed parameters per model
Downloads generated video files to specified output directory
Provides documented exit codes for automation error handling
limitations: Cannot blend results from multiple models in a single clip
Each model has specific duration caps (3-15 seconds depending on model)
Resolution ceilings vary: HappyHorse and Wan support 1080p, Seedance caps at 720p on this template

use_cases.title: Product Reveals with Identity Preservation
use_cases.description: Generate smooth camera movements around product images while maintaining brand-consistent packaging and label appearance. Use HappyHorse 1.0 I2V for geometry preservation and stable identity across multiple shots.

use_cases.title: Talking-Head Videos with Custom Voiceover
use_cases.description: Create spokesperson videos where the subject lip-syncs to your pre-recorded audio in multiple languages. Use Wan 2.7 with audio_url to drive lip-sync while describing the visual frame.

use_cases.title: Multi-Modal Brand Narratives
use_cases.description: Combine a character subject image, a reference video scene, and a reference audio voice tone into a single cohesive animation. Use Seedance 2.0 Pro for brand-consistent narrative clips.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Portrait Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this portrait with a gentle camera drift around the subject's face, subtle breathing motion, and identity-stable features.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user says: 'Make this photo move' or 'Animate this portrait'

prompt_templates.title: Product 360 Motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slow orbit around this product with macro detail reveal, smooth lighting shift, and packaging geometry preserved throughout.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user says: 'Make a product reveal video' or 'Show this from different angles'

prompt_templates.title: Lip-Sync to Voiceover
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a talking-head clip where the spokesperson in this image lip-syncs to the provided voiceover audio, medium close-up framing, warm key light from camera-left.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user says: 'Sync this image to this audio file' or 'Make this person speak this dialogue'

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the subject from image 1, apply the camera movement from video 1, and match the voice tone from audio 1. Medium close-up with warm lighting.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user says: 'Combine this image with this reference video and this audio'

output_examples.input: Animate this portrait with a gentle orbit around the face, subtle breathing, soft natural light
output_examples.output: Video clip showing smooth camera orbit around the subject with preserved facial identity and natural ambient audio

output_examples.input: Create a product reveal: slow push-in with packaging label always facing camera
output_examples.output: Video showing macro camera movement revealing product details with label geometry preserved throughout

output_examples.input: Make this spokesperson lip-sync to this 10-second voiceover for a training video
output_examples.output: Talking-head video with accurate lip-sync matching the provided audio track, 9:16 aspect ratio

best_practices: Front-load motion verbs in prompts: drift, orbit, dolly in, tilt, reveal to guide the AI toward primary action
Match duration parameter to audio length when using Wan 2.7 lip-sync; clip stays silent past audio duration
Use seed parameter for reproducible results when comparing prompt variations or generating multi-language variants
anti_patterns: Do not describe static elements the model already sees in the image; focus tokens on what should change or move
Avoid mixing radically different aesthetics (e.g., watercolor image with photoreal video reference) as output quality degrades
Do not request HappyHorse output aspect changes; the model preserves input aspect ratio only

faq.question: Which model should I use for portrait animation with identity preservation?
faq.answer: Use HappyHorse 1.0 I2V. It ranks #1 on Artificial Analysis Arena (Elo 1392) with strong facial fidelity and identity stability.

faq.question: How do I create lip-synced talking-head videos with my own audio?
faq.answer: Use Wan 2.7 text-to-video with the audio_url parameter. Pass your MP3/WAV (3-30s, max 15MB) and describe the visual frame in the prompt.

faq.question: Can I combine an image, reference video, and reference audio in one animation?
faq.answer: Yes. Use Seedance 2.0 Pro which accepts up to 9 image references (first is primary subject), 3 video references (2-15s each), and 3 audio references.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: HappyHorse preserves input aspect. Wan 2.7 supports 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4. Seedance 2.0 Pro supports adaptive, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 1:1, 21:9.

faq.question: How long can the generated videos be?
faq.answer: All models cap at 15 seconds. HappyHorse and Wan support 3-15s. Seedance supports 4-15s. Match your duration parameter to your content needs.

faq.question: What file formats does the CLI download?
faq.answer: The RunComfy CLI downloads generated videos from .runcomfy.net or .runcomfy.com URLs into your specified --output-dir.
